diagrams.title: Parse Tree (Syntax Tree) Example
diagrams.description: A visual representation of how a sentence 'The cat eats fish' is parsed into a hierarchical tree structure, showing how grammar rules break down the input into tokens and combine them into phrases.
elements: Terminal nodes (tokens: The, cat, eats, fish)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how parsers build hierarchical representations of text by applying grammar rules, fundamental to syntactic analysis in NLP

diagrams.title: Top-Down vs Bottom-Up Parsing Comparison
diagrams.description: A side-by-side comparison showing how Top-Down parsing (starts from root symbol S and expands to terminals) versus Bottom-Up parsing (starts from terminals and reduces to start symbol) process the same input.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the two fundamental parsing strategies, helping understand how different algorithms traverse grammar rules to analyze input

diagrams.title: Pushdown Automaton for Parsing
diagrams.description: A state machine diagram showing a Pushdown Automaton (PDA) that recognizes a simple grammar, demonstrating how a stack is used to track context during parsing.
elements: Transitions with input symbol and stack action
diagrams.purpose: Explains the theoretical foundation of parsing - how context-free grammars are recognized using stack-based computation, crucial for understanding parser design
